mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or threshold crossing fails to predict direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad-based domestic demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield positive 60d returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion characterized by peak industrial production and rising inflation, further increases in the index of industrial production signal capacity constraints and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle regimes, rising INDPRO correlates with positive 5d returns, or the mechanism's predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year increase in the consumer price index above 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5%, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing months with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive payroll surprises do not lead to higher 60d returns.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while real personal consumption expenditures accelerates indicates unsustainable consumer borrowing, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth < RSAFS MoM growth
failure_condition: The divergence condition fails to predict negative returns, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production falls below 90.0, it signals a severe contraction in manufacturing and industrial activity that reduces corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 65% of instances when INDPRO drops below 90.0, or the threshold crossing lacks predictive power in rolling windows.
